questions.question:
A CronJob diagnostics brief is incomplete and contains stale or unsafe scheduling guidance.

A ConfigMap named `cronjob-diagnostics-brief` already exists in namespace `cronjob-lab`. Repair it so that:
- `targetCronJob` is `log-pruner`
- `cronJobInventory` is `kubectl get cronjob log-pruner -n cronjob-lab -o wide`
- `scheduleCheck` is `kubectl get cronjob log-pruner -n cronjob-lab -o jsonpath='{.spec.schedule}'`
- `suspendCheck` is `kubectl get cronjob log-pruner -n cronjob-lab -o jsonpath='{.spec.suspend}'`
- `concurrencyPolicyCheck` is `kubectl get cronjob log-pruner -n cronjob-lab -o jsonpath='{.spec.concurrencyPolicy}'`
- `historyLimitsCheck` is `kubectl get cronjob log-pruner -n cronjob-lab -o custom-columns=SUCCESS:.spec.successfulJobsHistoryLimit,FAILED:.spec.failedJobsHistoryLimit`
- `jobTemplateCheck` is `kubectl get cronjob log-pruner -n cronjob-lab -o jsonpath='{.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec.restartPolicy}'`
- `safeManifestNote` is `confirm schedule, suspend=false, and history limits before changing the CronJob manifest`
- save the repaired ConfigMap manifest to `/tmp/exam/q1101/cronjob-diagnostics-brief.yaml`
- save a plain-text checklist to `/tmp/exam/q1101/cronjob-diagnostics-checklist.txt` with the sections `CronJob Inventory`, `Scheduling Checks`, and `Safe Manifest Review`

Do not delete the CronJob, do not create manual Jobs from it, and do not patch the schedule just to force an immediate run during this drill.

verification.description: The CronJob diagnostics brief ConfigMap uses the intended workload target, schedule, suspend, concurrency, history, and safe manifest guidance
The exported checklist contains the required sections and exact CronJob inventory, scheduling checks, and safe manifest review steps
The repaired ConfigMap manifest is exported and stale unsafe CronJob remediation guidance is removed
